My name is TechBot. I am a simple chatbot made using NLP.
I can answer basic questions about Python, Machine Learning and AI.
Python is a popular programming language used for AI, ML and data science.
Machine Learning is a way of teaching computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed.
Deep Learning is a type of Machine Learning that uses Neural Networks with many layers.
NLP stands for Natural Language Processing. It helps computers understand and process human language.
Natural Language Processing is a branch of AI that helps computers understand human language.
A Neural Network is a machine learning model inspired by the human brain. It has input, hidden and output layers.
CNN stands for Convolutional Neural Network. It is specially designed for working with images.
Convolutional Neural Network is a type of deep learning model used for image classification.
Data Science is the field of extracting useful insights from large amounts of data using statistics and ML.
Artificial Intelligence is the simulation of human intelligence in machines.
AI stands for Artificial Intelligence. It is the simulation of human intelligence in machines.
Supervised Learning is when we train a model using labeled data with both input and output.
Unsupervised Learning is when we train a model using only input data without any labels.
Overfitting is when a model performs well on training data but poorly on new data.
A dataset is a collection of data used to train and test machine learning models.
Linear Regression is a supervised ML algorithm used to predict continuous values like price or salary.
Logistic Regression is a supervised ML algorithm used to predict categories like yes or no, pass or fail.
Model Evaluation means checking how good our machine learning model is using metrics like accuracy and precision.
Accuracy means how many predictions were correct out of total predictions.
Precision means out of all predicted positive values how many were actually positive.
Recall means out of all actual positive values how many were correctly predicted.
